expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: historical analogs become required-before-ship gates
expectations.evidence: result.md explicitly addresses the case focus and ties it to a pre-ship gate: “Yes — the Phase 5b rubric explicitly enforces this as a required gate.” It further states: “Historical analogs that remain relevant must be rendered as explicit `[ANALOG-GATE]` entries…” and “The release recommendation must enumerate all `[ANALOG-GATE]` items that remain blocking before ship.”

expectations.text: [checkpoint_enforcement][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase5b
expectations.evidence: result.md aligns the finding to Phase 5b repeatedly: “Primary phase under test: Phase 5b (shipment-checkpoint review)” and “This benchmark’s focus is correctly placed in Phase 5b…” plus it references Phase 5b required outputs and sections (checkpoint audit + Release Recommendation) as the enforcement mechanism.
